text: Becky Biberstein Michael Green Frances Carr Tom Richhart Troy Cook Bobbie Jackson Kimberly Abney Sydnee Bradburn Lean ne Lee Jeff Tillman Mike Holland and Matt Norris Twelfth Sunday in Ordinary Time June 21 2026 This weekend June 20th 21st the Church celebrates the Twelfth Sunday in Ordinary Time Continuing the theme of discipleship this weekend's readings focus upon trust As disciples we are called to not only seek out God's will for our lives but to trust in it So often however we may not see the road ahead and are afraid to follow even as the Lord Himself has prepared the path for us Trusting in Him we find the courage to move forward On Wednesday we celebrate the Solemnity of the Nativity of St John the Baptist This is one of only three nativity feasts we celebrate each year the others being those of Our Lord and the Blessed Virgin Usually saints are celebrated on their heavenly birthday their death day but since St Johns birth is both a significant event in the history of salvation and recorded in the Scriptures we celebrate both his earthly and heavenly nativities Given its proximity to the summer solstice June 21st June 24th was often considered one the longest day of the year It is fitting therefore that St John's birthday falls as it does considering how he had preached to the crowds He must increase I must decrease John 330 From here on our days will be growing shorter a reminder from the seasons themselves that like St John we must decrease so that Christ may increase in our lives In addition to our liturgical celebrations our nation also marks this weekend as Father's Day Many countries throughout the world celebrate Father's Day in March on St Joseph's Day placing the foster father of our Lord as a model for fatherhood Though we might celebrate these two on different days we can still find in St Joseph our parish patron a true model of fatherly love and under whose prayerful protection we now entrust our fathers As such we wish a Happy Father's Day to all those in our parish who are fathers grandfathers godfathers uncles and all those who provide fatherly care to others in so many ways By living out your vocation of fatherhood you help to reveal to us glimpses of God the Father Thank you for living out your vocation Know of our love gratitude and prayers for you May St Joseph foster father of Jesus intercede for you before the throne of God Helping Hands Baby Bottle Campaign On Mother's Day we started the Baby Bottle Campaign in support of Helping Hands one of our local pregnancy and family resource centers As the campaign comes to an end baby bottles can be returned in the collection basket or to the Parish Office through next weekend After next weekend baby bottles can be returned directly to Helping Hands 116 Dustman Road Bluffton Thank you for your support of this local resource and ministry Ordinary Time Seeing Green Entering into Ordinary Time we begin to see a lot of green Liturgically green symbolizes hope and growth reflecting both the hidden years of Christ's life before His public ministry and the gradual growth of His Church after the Ascension For this reason green is the color most commonly seen throughout Ordinary Time The green vestment that I often wear for Sunday Mass was commissioned in memory of my grandparents Rocco Margaret William Johana Made for Ordinary Time it serves as a reminder of how I received the Faith passed down from my grandparents to my parents and through them to my siblings and me Like the growth of Ordinary Time the Faith was handed on in the ordinary moments of daily life through prayer sacrifice and faithful example On the back of the vestment is a pelican an ancient Christian symbol According to legend the pelican would feed its young with its own blood in times of famine For this reason Christians saw in the pelican an image of Christ who nourishes us with His Body and Blood in the Eucharist In this way the pelican reminds us of the sacrifices made by our families through whose daily acts of self giving Faith is passed from generation to generation Saturday June 20 400 4:45pm Confession 5:00pm Andrea Grover Sunday June 21 7:10am Rosary 7:30am Our others in your prayer for our parish priests Consider joining the Seven Sisters Apostolate To participate you would commit to praying one Holy Hour for our parish priest Fr David on ONE DAY OF THE WEEK of your choice Ideally this Holy Hour would be done in front of the Blessed Sacrament in the Church when possible Through the Seven Sisters Apostolate we will ensure that a Holy Hour is prayed each day of the week for our parish priest's benefit To join this apostolate or for more information contact Dianne at dhwitwergmail.com Become a CATECHIST Our Religious Education Program is always in need of volunteer catechistsco catechists and classroom assistants to help spread the Good News and to share the Catholic faith with our children Catechists are ordinary people like you who generously volunteer their time to share our Catholic faith values and traditions with our youngest generation of Christians and to walk besidebear witness to them They are positive role models who help children grow in faith spirituality and their relationship with God If you are interested in joining our Religious Education Program or would like more information please contact the Parish Office at
